knowledge_items.title: Blood-Based Biomarkers for Early Alzheimer's Disease Detection: A Cross-Sectional Study of Plasma pTau217 and NfL
key_findings: Plasma pTau217 shows 82% accuracy in distinguishing early Alzheimer's from healthy controls (n=120)
Neurofilament light (NfL) levels correlate with cognitive decline trajectory over 12 months
Single blood biomarker panel achieves AUC of 0.86 for amyloid PET positivity prediction
knowledge_items.methods_detail: Cross-sectional plasma biomarker analysis with amyloid PET validation, n=120 (60 early AD, 60 healthy controls)

knowledge_items.title: Multi-Modal Biomarker Fusion with Machine Learning for Alzheimer's Disease Classification
key_findings: Multi-modal fusion of plasma biomarkers, MRI volumetrics, and CSF measures achieves AUC of 0.94 for AD classification (n=500)
Machine learning integration improves diagnostic accuracy by 8–12% over any single biomarker modality
Plasma pTau217 + hippocampal volume + cognitive scores form the most predictive feature set
knowledge_items.methods_detail: Multi-modal biomarker fusion using gradient-boosted trees and deep learning, validated on 500 subjects from ADNI and independent cohort
limitations: High computational complexity limits clinical deployment
Cohort enriched for typical AD, may not generalize to atypical variants

knowledge_items.title: Blood and Imaging Biomarkers for Alzheimer's Disease: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis of 15 Prospective Studies
key_findings: Pooled sensitivity of plasma pTau217 for AD diagnosis across 15 studies is 87% (95% CI: 83–91%)
Combined blood + imaging biomarker panel achieves significantly higher diagnostic accuracy (AUC=0.93) than blood-only (AUC=0.84, p<0.001)
Heterogeneity in biomarker assay protocols remains a major barrier to clinical implementation
knowledge_items.methods_detail: Systematic review with random-effects meta-analysis of 15 prospective studies (total n=4,280) comparing blood and imaging biomarkers for AD diagnosis
limitations: High between-study heterogeneity in assay protocols
Publication bias may inflate pooled sensitivity estimates
Limited geographic diversity among included studies
